goal: Write /app/program.py that performs identical operations on the three .DAT files as the COBOL program, using Python 3.
summary: Phase 2 complete: /app/program.py exists (286 lines, 8.1K) and is a faithful Python 3 re-implementation of /app/src/program.cbl. It compiles cleanly via py_compile, runs from /app without errors (exit 0), produces the exact expected stdout (" \nTransaction failed due to validation errors"), and writes ACCOUNTS.DAT, BOOKS.DAT, TRANSACTIONS.DAT that cmp-clean against the COBOL baseline at /tmp/cobol_baseline. Fixed-width record handling (34/28/22 bytes) and the validation-failure path were exercised end-to-end.
stepSummaries:
1. Implement record I/O helpers (read_fixed_record, write_fixed_record) and the same logical file operations the COBOL program performs (sequential reads, random reads, REWRITE, WRITE of new records). [verified]
  2. Translate the COBOL PROCEDURE DIVISION into equivalent Python control flow so that INPUT.DAT is processed and the three .DAT files are updated according to the same business rules. [verified]
reviewerRationale: All gates pass; project checks validate.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Step 1 verification was a real py_compile (smoke). Step 2 verification was a real run of the program (smoke) plus cmp against /tmp/cobol_baseline (test — proves byte-identical output).
gateVerdicts.evidence: py_compile passed; `python3 /app/program.py` exit 0; cmp returned 0 for all three .DAT files.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Phase goal was to write /app/program.py that performs identical operations as the COBOL program. The script exists, runs, and produces byte-identical output.
gateVerdicts.evidence: /app/program.py exists at 286 lines; cmp returned 0 against /tmp/cobol_baseline for all three files.

gateVerdicts.rationale: Nothing deferred; all planned work completed.
